methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Dataminr
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $4.1B

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company founded by Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig, carries a current post-money valuation of $15.0B following $600M+ in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies and 1 public peer (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with top private comparables including Dataminr, OpenAI, xAI, and Anthropic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series F-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.3x applied to the $15.0B base valuation, implies $19.5B. Peer-median analysis across 8 comparable AI & Software companies at Series E/F stages yields a median valuation of $3.9B, though the peer set displays substantial dispersion (range: $1.1B to $250.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in capital efficiency, growth stage, and investor appetite within the sector.

The two methods produce materially divergent signals. Synthesizing across both approaches yields a triangulated range of $3.3B (low) to $22.4B (high), with a median of $11.7B. The $15.0B current valuation sits within this range, closer to the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the relevance of extreme outliers in the peer set (notably xAI at $250.0B) and the applicability of stage-progression mechanics in a market characterized by significant stage-specific valuation volatility. A narrower $11.7B−$19.5B range may better reflect consensus if outlier peers are discounted.
